summary: I read docs/project/adversarial-review-playbook.md in full and then ran `git diff main...HEAD --stat` (76 files, +870/-427) and full diffs of the funding/tenancy surface (pkg/funding/evaluate.go, pkg/funding/evaluate_test.go, pkg/funding/tenancy_r7_test.go, pkg/resolver/resolver.go, pkg/forecast/forecast.go, pkg/admission/admission.go, controllers/run_controller.go, cmd/scheduler/plugin/gang.go, api/v1/run_types.go) plus a grep for class-2 comment tells and a check of .autopilot-turn.log and any Status.Closed/RunPhaseFailed/CloseLease touches. The change deletes Run.Spec.Owner and derives the funding owner from the run's namespace via a new Evaluation.OwnerOf/deriveOwners with two conflict fail-safes. I confirmed mechanically (not adjudicated) that the diff reproduces the pre-flagged HIGH lead almost verbatim: deriveOwners' interior-tier exemption (`if _, isInterior := interior[owner]; isInterior { continue }`) skips the span-namespaces injectivity check for ANY owner appearing in any Budget.Spec.Parents, including one that is simultaneously directly bound (as a leaf Budget.Spec.Owner) in two namespaces — the new test TestInteriorTierExemptFromInjectivity only covers the benign case (pool with no direct leaf budgets of its own) and never constructs the dangerous case, which is a class-8 'test proves the safe subcase, not the dangerous one' pattern. I also confirmed the resolver.ownerOf fallback-to-namespace lead: when in.Evaluation is nil, pkg/resolver/resolver.go's new `ownerOf` helper returns run.Namespace directly as the funding-lottery bucket key, re-introducing a namespace-as-owner path in exactly the callers (reclaimUnfunded, runLottery) the amendment's stated purpose was to remove owner from being a raw field; the comment beside it asserts this is 'exactly what the owner would resolve to' — an unenforced claim (class 2) since no evaluation is being used to check it in that branch. I did not find any class-1 immortal-lease, class-3 last-writer-wins, class-4 signal-vs-reality, class-5 identity-coarsening, or class-7 cloned-Status.Closed hits in the touched files — the diff's mechanical rewiring of `run.Spec.Owner` to `ev.OwnerOf(run.Namespace)` across resolver/forecast/admission/run_controller/gang.go is uniform single-return-path substitution with no new early exits or phase/closure writes, and I explicitly grepped for RunPhaseFailed/RunPhaseComplete/CloseLease/Status.Closed churn in the touched controller files and found only one pre-existing unchanged phase literal in a test fixture. The .autopilot-turn.log hygiene issue (an accidentally committed local trust-dialog log line) is present and unrelated to correctness.

leads.why: The interior-tier exemption `if _, isInterior := interior[owner]; isInterior { continue }` skips the leaf-owner span-namespaces conflict check for any owner named in any Budget.Spec.Parents; an owner that is BOTH interior (named as a parent somewhere) and directly leaf-bound in two namespaces (via its own Budget.Spec.Owner in each) evades ConflictLeafOwnerSpansNamespaces entirely, so OwnerOf(nsA)==OwnerOf(nsB) with zero recorded conflicts and a Run in tenant-a can mint an Owned lease charged against tenant-b's envelope — an immortal, cross-tenant charge that is never flagged.

leads.taxonomyClass: 8 (test asserts the safe subcase, not the dangerous one)
leads.why: TestInteriorTierExemptFromInjectivity only constructs a pool budget with no leaf-owner collision of its own (two children naming the same parent); it never constructs the case named in the HIGH lead — an owner that is simultaneously a Parent AND directly bound via its own leaf Budget.Spec.Owner in two namespaces — so the exemption's dangerous branch is never exercised by any test in the diff, and a suite run would stay green even if the exemption is wrong.

The new `ownerOf` helper falls back to `run.Namespace` (raw, unvalidated by any Budget) when `in.Evaluation` is nil, so the reclaim/lottery bucket key silently becomes namespace-as-owner in that branch — exactly the coupling the amendment's stated purpose (owner is admin-derived, not a raw identifier) was meant to eliminate; the adjacent comment asserts this 'is exactly what the owner would resolve to' but nothing checks that claim against actual Budgets in this code path.

leads.taxonomyClass: 6 guard-before-predicate (benign but touches an auth-relevant check)
leads.why: promiseProvenanceValid's budget-list loop guard changed from `if b.Name != seg.BudgetName` to a combined `if b.Namespace != run.Namespace || b.Name != seg.BudgetName { continue }`, removing the prior explicit `b.Spec.Owner != run.Spec.Owner` ownership check entirely and replacing it with a namespace-equality filter placed before the envelope-name match predicate; worth confirming the combined filter is strictly equivalent to the deleted ownership check across all budget/namespace configurations, since a filter combining role/type and match conditions in one line is exactly the shape class 6 warns about even though order here does not obviously suppress a legitimate match.

The comment states 'the API server authenticates metadata.namespace, so it cannot be forged' as the justification for deleting the spec.owner validation; this is an assertion about a property enforced entirely outside this file (by the API server/RBAC), not by any code in this diff, and its correctness for every caller (including any future direct-etcd or admission-bypass path) is not checked anywhere in the change.
A local trust-dialog diagnostic line was accidentally committed to the branch; harmless to funding correctness but should be removed before merge per the pre-flagged lead list.
